steps.title: Separate the two measurements
steps.content: The sentence reports one temperature comparison before construction and another after construction. Each comparison has its own subject and verb, so the two independent clauses need a full stop or another correct independent-clause boundary.

steps.title: Reject run-ons and splices
steps.content: Choice A runs the clauses together with no punctuation. Choice B leaves the same run-on and misplaces a comma after after. Choice D uses only a comma to join two independent clauses. The answer is C.
